paragraphs.body: The Scent of Nostalgia “MAMAAA!” An airy, childlike voice rang out from the hall, as tiny feet pitter-pattered down the hallway. “Mama, mama, mama!” I shouted melodiously in little spurts, as I flounced over towards the wooden bathroom door. “Yes, Booda?”, She purred lovingly, glancing over to me. There was my mama in all her glory, dressed in sleek black leather pants, adorned with an equally as stunning black leather jacket, and a simple white top to match. I looked up at her, as she took a carefully manicured hand to rummage through her makeup bag. She peered into the bag, stained with mysterious liquids and powders, and seemed to be deep in thought. I could tell from the furrow in her eyebrows, which was followed by a perplexed facial expression. All the while, I shimmied my way into the bathroom, and behind my mother’s shapely legs. Her other hand reached down to caress the top of my head. I angled myself on the edge of the smooth porcelain bathtub edge, swinging one of my legs over the side. My feet felt the residue of cold water, and I recoiled, swiping my feet towards a dry patch of tub. This was the perfect angle to watch mama. It seemed as if she hadn’t found her object yet- and she heaved heavily. “Booda, did you take my cherry gloss? I want to use it right now…” She fixed her eyes on me. Guiltily, I stood up, pitter-pattering towards my room, to grab the small tube-shaped container from my wind breaker pocket. “Here Mama…”, I handed the gloss to mama. She smiled, before re-applying her coat of gloss. “Thank you, baby.” She caressed my head, before continuing her private feminine affairs.

annotations.text: Guiltily, I stood up, pitter-pattering towards my room, to grab the small tube-shaped container from my wind breaker pocket. “Here Mama…”, I handed the gloss to mama. She smiled, before re-applying her coat of gloss. “Thank you, baby.” She caressed my head, before continuing her private feminine affairs.
annotations.comment: This sets your reader up for the sense of "guilt" or "danger" in taking the lip gloss. Nice.

annotations.context_left: of gloss. “Thank you, baby.” She caressed my head, before continuing her private feminine affairs.

paragraphs.body: This object consists of a singular tube of cherry lip gloss. The outside is rounded, it’s long, and tubular shaped. Holding it in my hands, I can feel a thin layer of plastic separating the outer coating and the true surface of the tube of gloss. The top of the tube is a gold color and feels slightly sticky to the touch. The end of the container is fan shaped; its square edges dotted with tiny ridges alongside the ends. The text is printed, bold and a hot pink, “HYRDATING, Promotes NOURISHING, Contains MULTIVITAMINS.” The back displays the ingredients, chemicals, oils, and flavorings. “Ruby Kisses”, is fully displayed on the back, along with “CHERRY.” The plastic is clear- you can see the gloss sliding around the insides. When the cap is unscrewed, the balmy nostalgic scent of cherry wafts in the air. A bead of gloss bubbles at the top, which I would apply quickly across my bottom lip. Sometimes, when I screw the cap back on, the gloss bubbles up at the sides of the cap. I would take my pinky, spreading it across my bottom lip. Nothing gets left behind. When I finished the application, the ritual would continue to commence. Now left with the task to evenly coat my lips in the gloss, but to a subtle extent- depending on the day, and the look. Once fully coated, I would find the nearest reflective surface, to inspect my handiwork. Once I was satisfied, the tube of nostalgia would be tucked into the nearest pocket of mine, to be secured until the next usage. Sometimes the container would be lost, perhaps from being shoved far within my bookbag pocket, or stuffed away in the corners of a purse. When hunting for my mother’s lip-gloss, her purse would be the first place I went to begin my hunt. I’d stuff my hand deep within the leather, plucking off various bits of debris and unwrapped gum sticks that crumbled, and were left to sediment at the bottom of the fabric lined bag. Sifting my small fingers around until I found the tube, the object. Then, I would inspect. Sometimes, the flavors would be mango, mint, strawberry, my mother liked options, she liked to be free to choose whatever she wanted. If I didn’t find the flavor I wanted, the gloss would be thrust back into the bag until further notice- I’d keep mental note of the location as a placeholder in case my hunt for the cherry flavor didn’t become fruitful, and continue my search.

paragraphs.body: The scent of cherries always lingered as my mother kissed my cheek, remaining even after her tears stained my sheets. When I was younger, I would associate her blood with the smudged gloss on the surface of the walls, from the impacts. I would be too scared to touch it, and would stare from the corner of the hallway, as if it burned a hole through the white plastered walls. Blood. Bloo, Bluh, Blood. Blood is an awfully striking word, isn’t it? It’s roots devolving from a Nordic background, something I don’t know much about. Old English plays a role into this as well- blod. Consisting of fluid flowing through the arteries and veins. It’s known as the ‘fluid of life’, in many cultures and backgrounds. So how come every time I’ve interacted with this ‘fluid of life’, have I experienced near death situations? If this word is so negative, how could there be any light in them? Back in middle school, I tenderly cared for many plants. Under my clumsy care, most of them perished due to inadequate conditions, such as lack of water, the wrong soil, or my inability to sense when a seedling needed watering. I would run fro to fro from my bathroom sink to my room, carrying a greasy Chinese food cup full of tap water, running back towards my bedroom to dump the contents into a small pot which I poured all my love into, and doted upon feverishly. Despite the multiple attempts of accidental drownings of this little plant, one day- I came home to see little milky white buds sprouting on the thin green stalk of the plant. Whilst I had no idea what plant this was, and to know if this was a positive sign- I continued to water it. It bloomed. That little plant bloomed, and spread, and prospered until a misguided room cleaning session brought its thriving nature to a swift end. I haven’t even dubbed a name upon the plant, and there its life went, as quickly as it had begun.

paragraphs.body: The suffix of Blood consists of “Bhel-“, which means to thrive, or to bloom. Despite my mother being flooded by violence, her resilience always shone through. The tube of cherry lip gloss I presume gave her comfort as much as it gave younger me comfort, for its scent reminded us both of better days, hot summer walks in the city, and the womanly primping of both of us in the mirror as we prepared ourselves to be presentable for the day. Nowadays whenever I have an important event, or simply when I need a touch up, my cherry lip-gloss is a first. While we’re on the topic of cherries, I’ve always wondered why my mom wore cherry gloss, rather than any other flavor. Subconsciously, I know fruit has many hidden meanings I’m playing too far into. The deep ruby red complexion of it being akin to blood droplets, the sharp distinct taste as we bite into the fruit, all of it can be related to that word. Blood, blooming, everchanging. Baked into many different desserts, cherry pie, cherry cake, cherry tarts, cherry cobbler. Everchanging, blooming, and free.

Baked into many different desserts, cherry pie, cherry cake, cherry tarts, cherry cobbler. Everchanging, blooming, and free.
